% Generated by build_book.py — do not edit by hand.
% Part 9: After
% Letters: 2

% --- L101 -- L101_1944-10-11_from-don-chapin ---
\begin{fromothers}[%
  id={L101},
  date={1944-10-11},
  datelabel={October 11, 1944},
  location={FROM Don Chapin (U.S.S. Rowe DD-563) TO Gene in Lexington, Ky.},
  status={transcribed\_draft},
  statuslabel={transcribed\_draft},
  pagecount={5},
  returnaddress={Don. Chapin (Cox)\\U.S.S. Rowe D.D. 563\\\% Fleet P.O.\\San Francisco\\California}%
]
\salutation{Dear Gene,}
Well Gene I guess it's about time that I drop you a few lines. Should have done so sooner but have been sort of busy you know.

How have you been? I hope that you are getting along alright because I can imagine how it is not to be feeling so good.

Received a letter from my wife the other day and she had enclosed the letter you had written to her. Well I guess you know what kind of a spot that puts me in. I've tried to think it over Gene and from the sound of thing I'd say that you have lost your faith lately. I bet you kid she is my wife and I would be human if I said that was the kind of a letter I wanted a buddy of mine to write her. It was here in radio's and I let you live \& nothing but I think you were in the wrong did not telling me that it was in no shape to ship so you let it stay in the hospital. I know I wouldn't have and I'm sure O.K. wouldn't have thought no more about it.

As for buying for it you know darn well that I wouldn't let you do anything of the kind. I know your letter upset her, Gene and I feel bad about the whole works. I don't know if you know it or not but she has been and still is a pretty sick girl and I worry about her an awfully lot. Between that \& this damn war I'm in a sweat all the time. The longer I stay out here the more discouraged I get. It's still rough and will not likely get rougher.

I haven't heard nothing about Jack but I'm still hoping to run across him. Have had a little dope on our friends but I can't tell you what it is as yet.

Right now I'm on the M.A.A. force and all I can say about that is that it's an ungrateful job. I still haven't gotten like Maudsley yet anyway.

Well it's almost midnight so I had better close. Hope to hear from you Gene and give my regards to Joan. Oh yes as one favor to me try not think to hard of B.S. Maybe someday we can all meet as straighten things out right. Good luck kid.
\signature{Your old buddy,\\Don.\\Donald V. Chapin}
\end{fromothers}

% --- L102 -- L102_1944-12-01_from-parkhurst-wife ---
\begin{fromothers}[%
  id={L102},
  date={1944-12-01},
  datelabel={December 1, 1944},
  location={FROM Mary L. Parkhurst (Mt. Vernon, MO) TO Gene in Lexington, Ky.},
  status={transcribed\_draft},
  statuslabel={transcribed\_draft},
  pagecount={5},
  returnaddress={Mrs. John S. Parkhurst\\Mount Vernon, Missouri\\R.F.D. \#2}%
]
\salutation{Dear Mr. Lankford,}
Yes I did recognize your name Johnny, I call Jack, Johnny — Johnny has mentioned you, \& your picture is in Johnny's album of Hawaii several times. I was very much surprised to hear from you.

Yes I know you \& Johnny were shipmates for a long time. Johnny is a radar-operator now — while in Pearl Harbor sometime ago Johnny sent a Firecontrol man from off the U.S.S. New Orleans \& learned you were discharged \& that Chapin has been transferred to another ship.

Johnny told me to send you \& also Chapin our wedding announcements. I'll tell Johnny you married Joan — I knew you were engaged a long time ago — Johnny told me.

I write Johnny everyday \& I'll tell him about Joan \& hearing from you. Oh yes — I don't know the Firecontrol man's name that Johnny saw. He, Johnny saw our resident there at the same time he was there at Pearl Harbor.

Of course it isn't asking too much to get Johnny's address. I know he will be very glad to hear from you. His address —

\begin{addressblock}
Mr. John S. Parkhurst, "F" Division\\
U.S.S. Massachusetts\\
\% Fleet Post Office\\
San Francisco, California
\end{addressblock}

I know you do have a lot of news to tell him. I'll send your address to Johnny today. Yes I'll tell Johnny about Chapin (his picture is in Johnny's album too) \& that Chapin has a baby.

Where Johnny is at present I can't say — but I think he is in the Philippines now.

It is going on 9 weeks since I heard from Johnny but I look for a letter everyday. We, Johnny \& I were in Bremerton Wash. May 29 to July 10, this year. Then my Johnny had to go back but was suppose to get a transfer in not longer than 3 months from July. We hope he will get a transfer to Wash. D.C. to go to school anytime now. I'm hoping Johnny will be home for Christmas, not hearing lately I hope means Johnny is on his way home. Johnny is a radar operator Petty Officer 3rd Class now. Officer Riley (the division officer) said Johnny was the best operator on the ship. Johnny shot down a "Betty" Jap bomber too.

Yes I recognized who the picture was of. I would like to have it back if I'm sending it back to you. I have Johnny's album he brought home in 1941.

The New Orleans was due in Bremerton when I came home — the bow was blown off. I suppose you have read the book Chaplin Forgy wrote about the New Orleans. I saw Johnny's ship in, Bremerton. It's a beauty.

I hope sometime Johnny \& I will meet you \& Joan. I'm glad for you two that you are home. I know how very awful it is without my Johnny home.

I was glad to give you Johnny's address as I know he will be glad to hear from you. I hope you soon hear from him. The best of luck to you \& Joan.
\signature{Johnny's Wife,\\Mrs. Parkhurst}
\end{fromothers}
